title: cacalia elaeagnoides
intro: Cacalia elaeagnoides, also known as the Elephant's Ear, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Cacalia elaeagnoides can be used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
